channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Pelicans players, coach grieve those killed in New Orleans attack as they play in Miami
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Trey Murphy III knew his mother was in downtown New Orleans to ring in the new year. His chef was there as well. And when Murphy heard about the tragedy that happened on Bourbon Street, he was scared for their safety. The word eventually came: They were fine.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Aaron Rodgers says Jets' season finale vs. Dolphins could be his last NFL game
item.description: Aaron Rodgers was questioned by reporters on Wednesday whether the Jets' season finale against the Dolphins could be his final NFL game, and he said 'of course'
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pistons guard Jaden Ivey sustains leg injury in victory over the Magic
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Detroit guard Jaden Ivey sustained what appeared to be a severe injury to his lower left leg in the fourth quarter of Detroit's victory over the Orlando Magic on Wednesday night. Orlando's Cole Anthony slipped and fell as he and Ivey went for the ball.
item.dc:creator: DAVE HOGG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio State stomps Oregon in Rose Bowl beatdown to advance to CFP semifinal
item.description: Ohio State blew out Oregon 41-21 with a 34-point first half in the Rose Bowl to end their newest conference rival's inaugural season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Targeting no-call at Peach Bowl raises more questions about disputed rule
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Peach Bowl referee declined to call a targeting penalty late in the fourth quarter for a helmet-to-helmet hit by Texas safety Michael Taaffe that could've given Arizona State a chance to kick a winning field goal in the College Football Playoff quarterfinals Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Eagles coach Nick Sirianni set to rest Saquon Barkley in finale and end chance at NFL rushing record
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Saquon Barkley had a heart-to-heart talk with his dad that was more coated in heartbreak than hope over the Philadelphia Eagles running back's pursuit of the NFL single-season rushing record.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saquon Barkley and family not happy about Eagles sitting him with chance to break NFL rushing record
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles player Saquon Barkley addressed his team's decision to rest him in the last game of the season, skipping a chance to break a league record.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas survives double OT scare from Arizona State in college football playoff thriller
item.description: Texas defensive back Andrew Mukuba hauled in an interception to clinch a college football playoff win after a shocking effort from underdog Arizona State.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: University of Georgia student 'critically injured' in New Orleans attack ahead of Sugar Bowl game
item.description: University of Georgia president Jere W. Morehead confirmed that one student was "critically injured" during an apparent terror attack in New Orleans on Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gauff and Fritz put US in United Cup semifinals. Kazakhstan beats Germany to advance
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — Coco Gauff and Taylor Fritz powered the U.S., tournament winner two years ago, into the semifinals of the United Cup mixed teams tennis event on Wednesday. In the first singles match, Gauff had a 7-6 (4), 6-2 win over China's Zhang Shuai.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: LeBron James' youngest son Bryce commits to Arizona
item.description: Bryce James, son of NBA star LeBron James, is heading to Arizona. Bryce James announced his decision Wednesday on Instagram and his father had a quick response.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sugar Bowl in New Orleans postponed after deadly truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame has been postponed after a truck plowed into a New Year’s crowd about a mile away early Wednesday, killing at least 10 people.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Officials postpone Sugar Bowl in the wake of apparent terror attack on Bourbon Street
item.description: The 2025 Sugar Bowl in New Orleans has been postponed just hours before kickoff Wednesday after at least 10 people were killed and dozens more were injured in what federal officials are investigating as an act of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Notre Dame calls on fans to 'join us in prayer’ following apparent terror attack ahead of Sugar Bowl
item.description: The University of Notre Dame is calling on fans in New Orleans ahead of the Sugar Bowl to join in prayer following a "senseless act of violence" that left at least 10 people dead and injured more than two dozen others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eagles head coach Nick Sirianni says he'll meet with staff, players about Saquon Barkley's chase for record
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles have nothing to play for, but Saquon Barkley is 101 yards away from breaking Eric Dickerson's single-season rushing record.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No. 5 Penn State bottles up Jeanty, beats No. 8 Boise State 31-14 in Fiesta Bowl
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Penn State defenders clogged every hole, stretched out every outside run, closed with ferocity. Everywhere that Ashton Jeanty turned, he ran into Nittany Lions. The Heisman Trophy runner-up still went over 100 yards, but he sure had to work for it.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arizona State extends coach Kenny Dillingham 5 more years, AP source says
item.description: Arizona State coach Kenny Dillingham has agreed to a five-year contract extension on the eve of the Sun Devils' College Football Playoff quarterfinal game, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lenny Randle, major leaguer repeatedly in the spotlight for 12 seasons, dies at 75
item.description: MURRIETA, Calif. (AP) — Lenny Randle, a big league player for 12 seasons who spoke five languages, performed stand-up comedy, was dubbed “The Most Interesting Man in Baseball” and was suspended for punching his Texas Rangers manager, has died. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers agree to new deal with Teoscar Hernández: report
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers fans will need to stock up on sunflower seeds after the team agreed to a new deal with fan favorite slugger Teoscar Hernández. Hernández will return to the Dodgers on a new 3-year, $66 million deal, according to ESPN's Jeff Passan.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5
